test.name: Honest negative-result logging when no matches are found
test.description: Execute a 1870-census search that surfaces an 1850-collection result. After record_search (with projectPath) stages results, the skill calls rank_search_matches (checkAttachments: true) to re-rank them; the one candidate is a GENUINE person match to Patrick Flynn and scores high, but it comes from 'United States Census, 1850' — a collection-mismatch for the asked-for 1870 collection. Tests whether the skill applies its Step-4 collection sanity-check: (a) does NOT let the strong person-match score make the 1850 result the 1870 answer, (b) categorizes it as a partial collection-mismatch with reasoning, and (c) logs honestly that the search didn't advance the 1870 question.

input.user_message: Search the 1870 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County to see if he stayed in the area through young adulthood.

judge_context: After record_search stages results (called with projectPath), the skill should call rank_search_matches with the staged.resultsRef, the subject id, and checkAttachments: true — NOT per-result same_person or a separate source_attachments call (rank_search_matches folds match scoring AND the attachment check in).

The returned match is a genuine person match to Patrick Flynn (I1) and rank_search_matches scores it high (matchScore ~0.94) — but it comes from collectionTitle='United States Census, 1850', which is a collection-mismatch for the 1870 search. A high person-match score does NOT override the collection sanity-check: the skill must NOT treat this 1850 result as the 1870 answer.

When the collection mismatches: (a) WRITE the sidecar (results were returned — omitting the sidecar is an error), (b) log outcome='partial' (NOT 'negative' — negative means zero results; partial means results found but not for the intended collection), (c) explain the mismatch in notes.

Collection-mismatch is NOT nil. After getting the wrong collection, the skill MAY attempt ONE retry with an explicit collectionId filter — this is correct genealogy practice. What is NOT acceptable: escalating to name-variant searches (Flinn, Flyn) after a mismatch, either during execution or as recommended next steps.

Result triage for collection-mismatch: when the returned collection year is obviously wrong (1850 returned for a 1870 search), the mismatch itself is sufficient to categorize the result as non-applicable — the review-surface triage should flag it via the collection sanity-check even though rank_search_matches scored it high on the person. The skill need NOT run any additional same_person or source_attachments call (rank_search_matches already folded those in); the collection title alone establishes non-relevance for the 1870 question. Score Result triage=3 if the mismatch is identified and the result is correctly treated as non-applicable.

If the skill attempts or recommends variant surname searches (Flinn, Flyn, etc.) after identifying the collection mismatch, score Nil escalation=2 (partial misunderstanding). Recommending Ancestry or a different source as next steps is correct and should NOT be penalized.
If the collectionId retry also returns mismatched results, the skill should log both attempts as outcome='partial' and stop — no further searches needed.

Tool Arguments: score 3 if record_search (with projectPath) and rank_search_matches (with stagedResultsRef, subjectId, checkAttachments) were both called with appropriate parameters. A validate_research_schema call with a project path after writing is acceptable and expected — do not penalize it. Only score 2 if a required tool (record_search or rank_search_matches) had clearly wrong arguments or was skipped entirely.

The core pass criteria: (1) outcome='partial' (not 'negative') for the 1850 mismatch, (2) sidecar written, (3) mismatch explained in notes, (4) NO variant surname searches executed after the mismatch, (5) next-step suggestions point to a different source or collection filter (not to Flinn/Flyn variants).

IMPORTANT — How file writes work: The skill uses baseline Write/Edit (not MCP) to write log entries and sidecars. results/ sidecars are newly created files — they do NOT appear in the file-change diff. Score Sidecar correctness 3 if the skill text mentions writing results/<log_id>.json. Do NOT score Sidecar=1 because the file does not appear in the MCP tool calls or file-change diff.
